untranslatable_reason: Signal is a contract rolling strategy for futures (RAP - Roll Around Period), not a price/indicator-based trading signal. Key untranslatable elements: (1) 'Roll Around Period' timing is a contract calendar event, not a technical indicator; (2) 'roll portion of contracts' requires position management logic not expressible in indicator conditions; (3) 'volatility at end of RAP' requires forward-looking volatility comparison unavailable in standard indicators; (4) 'daily traded spread volume constraint' requires spread-specific volume data not in allowed indicators; (5) The w% parameter is an investor choice variable, not a computable condition. This rule requires contract expiration calendars, position management, and spread analytics beyond the scope of OHLCV-based technical indicators.
translation_notes: Original table appears to be performance data (P&L or returns across different w% values and time periods), not actionable signal logic. Signal is fundamentally a portfolio/position management rule for futures rolling, best implemented via custom contract management logic rather than technical indicator conditions.
